name: French Meatball Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Add rice to 1 cup of water. Bring to a boil for 5 minutes, then drain well.
2. Blend the rice, ground beef, egg (raw beaten), grated onion, minced garlic, parsley, and nutmeg until smooth.
3. Form the mixture into small meatballs.
4. Add the red wine to the broth and bring to a boil.
5. Drop the meatballs into the hot broth one at a time. Bring back to a boil.
6. Reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes.
7. Season with salt and pepper.
8. Microwave: Add rice to 1 cup of salted water. Bring to a boil, then hold for 5 minutes until drained well.
9. Combine the meatball ingredients as described above.
10. Bring wine and broth to a boil. Drop the meatballs into the hot broth one at a time.
11. Return to a boil and hold for an additional 10 minutes.
12. Season with salt and pepper.
Food Exchange per serving: 1 MEAT EXCHANGE + 1 STARCH/BREAD EXCHANGE
CAL: 71
Source: The Complete Diabetic Cookbook by Mary Jane Finsand
Brought to you and yours by Nancy O'Brion and her Meal-Master
